content_en:
Al-Shamail al-Muhammadiyyah (The Prophetic Characteristics) is one of the most intimate and beloved works of hadith literature. Compiled by the master hadith scholar Imam Abu Isa Muhammad ibn Isa al-Tirmidhi (209–279 AH / 824–892 CE), it gathers 415 narrations describing in precise detail the physical appearance, daily habits, worship, speech, dress, food, sleep, laughter, and character of the Prophet Muhammad ﷺ. Al-Tirmidhi was among the foremost students of Imam al-Bukhari and one of the compilers of the Six Canonical Hadith Collections (al-Kutub al-Sittah), making his scholarly credentials among the highest in the science of hadith.

Unlike the broader hadith collections, the Shamail is focused exclusively on the person of the Prophet ﷺ — not his rulings, his battles, or his theological teachings, but him as a human being known and loved by his Companions. The narrations come predominantly from those closest to him: his wife Aisha (may Allah be pleased with her), his cousin and son-in-law Ali ibn Abi Talib, his servant Anas ibn Malik, his Companion al-Bara' ibn Azib, and dozens of others who observed him directly. Through their eyes, the reader encounters the Prophet ﷺ in vivid, human detail — the seal between his shoulders, the way he smiled, how he sat at the dinner table, what he said before sleeping.

The Shamail has been the subject of numerous commentaries across the centuries, the most celebrated being that of Imam Ibn Allan al-Siddiqi (d. 1057 AH) and the widely taught commentary of Imam al-Bajuri (d. 1277 AH). In the modern era, a much-studied explanation by Imam Yusuf al-Nabahani and a contemporary commentary by Sheikh Muhammad Zakariyya al-Kandhlawi have made the text accessible to new generations. It is studied in traditional Islamic institutions worldwide as part of the standard hadith curriculum.

For students of Ahl us-Sunnah wal-Jama'ah, the Shamail serves a dual purpose: it is both a work of rigorous hadith scholarship and a book of spiritual enrichment. Reading it is considered an act of love for the Prophet ﷺ, and many scholars have noted that familiarity with its contents deepens one's connection to the Sunnah, makes the prophetic example feel vivid and present, and cultivates the characteristic of love (mahabbah) which the scholars consider a pillar of faith. The Prophet ﷺ is not an abstract figure in this text — he is a man whose walk, whose fragrance, whose humor, and whose grief the reader comes to know.

Students approaching the Shamail should read it alongside an established commentary, as some narrations require contextual explanation regarding the historical circumstances of the early Muslim community. The hadith gradings vary — al-Tirmidhi himself notes the status of many narrations — and a teacher can help navigate these distinctions. Above all, the Shamail should be read slowly, with contemplation, allowing each description to deepen reverence and love for the best of creation, peace and blessings be upon him.
content_id:
Al-Syama'il al-Muhammadiyyah (Sifat-Sifat Nabi Muhammad) adalah salah satu karya literatur hadis yang paling intim dan dicintai. Dikumpulkan oleh pakar hadis terkemuka Imam Abu Isa Muhammad ibn Isa al-Tirmidzi (209–279 H / 824–892 M), buku ini menghimpun 415 riwayat yang menggambarkan secara tepat penampilan fisik, kebiasaan harian, ibadah, ucapan, pakaian, makanan, tidur, tawa, dan karakter Nabi Muhammad ﷺ. Al-Tirmidzi adalah salah satu murid terkemuka Imam al-Bukhari dan salah satu penyusun Enam Koleksi Hadis Kanonik (al-Kutub al-Sittah), menjadikan kredensial keilmuannya di antara yang tertinggi dalam ilmu hadis.

Tidak seperti koleksi-koleksi hadis yang lebih luas, Syama'il berfokus eksklusif pada diri Nabi ﷺ — bukan hukum-hukumnya, peperangan-peperangannya, atau ajaran-ajaran teologisnya, melainkan beliau sebagai manusia yang dikenal dan dicintai oleh para Sahabatnya. Riwayat-riwayat itu sebagian besar berasal dari mereka yang paling dekat dengan beliau: istrinya Aisyah (semoga Allah meridhainya), sepupu dan menantunya Ali ibn Abi Thalib, pelayannya Anas ibn Malik, Sahabatnya al-Bara' ibn Azib, dan puluhan lainnya yang langsung mengamati beliau. Melalui mata mereka, pembaca menjumpai Nabi ﷺ dalam detail yang hidup dan manusiawi — meterai di antara bahunya, cara beliau tersenyum, cara beliau duduk di meja makan, apa yang beliau ucapkan sebelum tidur.

Syama'il telah menjadi subjek berbagai komentar sepanjang abad, yang paling dirayakan adalah komentar Imam Ibn Allan al-Siddiqi (w. 1057 H) dan komentar yang banyak diajarkan dari Imam al-Bajuri (w. 1277 H). Di era modern, penjelasan yang banyak dipelajari oleh Imam Yusuf al-Nabahani dan komentar kontemporer oleh Syaikh Muhammad Zakariyya al-Kandhlawi telah membuat teks ini dapat diakses oleh generasi-generasi baru. Ia dipelajari di lembaga-lembaga Islam tradisional di seluruh dunia sebagai bagian dari kurikulum hadis standar.

Bagi pelajar Ahl us-Sunnah wal-Jama'ah, Syama'il melayani tujuan ganda: ia sekaligus merupakan karya hadis yang ketat dan buku pengayaan spiritual. Membacanya dianggap sebagai amalan cinta kepada Nabi ﷺ, dan banyak ulama mencatat bahwa keakraban dengan isinya memperdalam keterhubungan seseorang dengan Sunnah, membuat contoh Nabawi terasa hidup dan hadir, serta memupuk sifat cinta (mahabbah) yang oleh para ulama dianggap sebagai pilar iman. Nabi ﷺ bukanlah sosok abstrak dalam teks ini — beliau adalah seorang manusia yang cara jalannya, aromanya, humornya, dan kesedihannya menjadi dikenal oleh pembaca.

Pelajar yang mendekati Syama'il sebaiknya membacanya bersama komentar yang mapan, karena beberapa riwayat memerlukan penjelasan kontekstual mengenai keadaan historis komunitas Muslim awal. Penilaian hadis bervariasi — al-Tirmidzi sendiri mencatat status banyak riwayat — dan seorang guru dapat membantu menavigasi perbedaan-perbedaan ini. Di atas segalanya, Syama'il hendaknya dibaca secara perlahan, dengan perenungan, membiarkan setiap gambaran memperdalam rasa hormat dan cinta kepada makhluk terbaik, shalawat dan salam atas beliau.
